sanskrit: ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते । आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः ॥
hindiTranslation: क्योंकि हे कुन्तीनन्दन  जो इन्द्रियों और विषयोंके संयोगसे पैदा होनेवाले भोग सुख हैं, वे आदिअन्तवाले और दुःखके ही कारण हैं ।  अतः विवेकशील मनुष्य उनमें रमण नहीं करता ॥
englishTranslation: The pleasures born from contact with the senses are only sources of suffering. They have a beginning and an end, O Arjuna, and the wise do not delight in them.
essence: Passing pleasure loses its power when its ending is seen.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Pleasures that come from the senses do not last and often lead to suffering. A clear-minded person does not get absorbed in them.
simpleInsight: You can enjoy life without being pulled around by every passing pleasure.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen before the war. Krishna continues teaching him how to see clearly: sense-based pleasures are temporary, and their sweetness hides the suffering they bring. A steady mind stops chasing them.
modernRelevance: You open your phone for one quick check and lose twenty minutes, then feel worse. The pull was brief; the aftertaste was not. Clear seeing breaks that loop.
translationLiteral: ये (these) / हि (indeed) / संस्पर्शजा (born from contact) / भोगाः (pleasures) / दुःखयोनयः (sources of suffering) / एव (only) / ते (they) । / आद्यन्तवन्तः (having beginning and end) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / न (not) / तेषु (in them) / रमते (delights) / बुधः (the wise person)
insight: It shifts attention from chasing stimulation to seeing its cost before you reach for it.

quoteVariants.text: Sense-pleasures begin and end. The wise do not cling to what breeds suffering.
Begin and end. Do not cling.
What flashes pleasure often plants suffering.
